2606.23238.title: HOLMES: Evaluating Higher-Order Logical Reasoning in LLMs
2606.23238.paper_summary: Logical reasoning is essential for reliable AI, yet existing benchmarks are largely first-order-logic-centric, focusing on object-level deduction over fixed predicates. This misses many realistic scenarios where models must reason over rules, predicates, functions, constraints, and decision procedures themselves. We introduce HOLMES (Higher-Order Logic Meets real-world Explainable Symbolic reasoning), the first real-world benchmark for higher-order symbolic reasoning in LLMs, containing 1379 instances. Built on higher-order logic, HOLMES pairs natural-language problems with HOL formalizations, ground-truth answers, verifiable reasoning traces, and fine-grained controllable reasoning factors across law and finance. Experiments show that current LLMs still struggle on HOLMES, with an average accuracy of only 50.64% and the best model reaching 59.54%. Our analyses further reveal that high final-answer accuracy can mask shortcut reasoning in conflict-resolution settings, while performance drops sharply under scope-conditioned and compositional reasoning. These findings identify higher-order symbolic reasoning as a key bottleneck for building reliable and verifiable LLMs. The project code and dataset are publicly available at https://github.com/wuyucheng2002/HOLMES.
2606.23238.paper_summary_zh: 邏輯推理對於可靠的人工智慧至關重要，然而現有的基準主要以一階邏輯為中心，專注於固定謂詞的物件層級推導。這忽略了許多現實場景，在這些場景中，模型必須對規則、謂詞、函數、約束和決策程序本身進行推理。我們介紹了HOLMES（高階邏輯與現實世界可解釋符號推理的結合），這是LLMs中首個針對高階符號推理的現實世界基準，包含1379個實例。HOLMES基於高階邏輯，將自然語言問題與HOL形式化、真實答案、可驗證的推理痕跡以及法律和金融領域的細粒度可控推理因素相結合。實驗顯示，當前的LLMs在HOLMES上仍然面臨挑戰，平均準確率僅為50.64%，最佳模型的準確率達到59.54%。我們的分析進一步揭示，高最終答案的準確率可能掩蓋了在衝突解決情境中的捷徑推理，而在範圍條件和組合推理下，性能則急劇下降。這些發現將高階符號推理確定為構建可靠和可驗證的LLMs的關鍵瓶頸。項目代碼和數據集可在https://github.com/wuyucheng2002/HOLMES上公開獲取。
2606.23238.author: Yucheng Wu et.al.
2606.23238.authors: Yucheng Wu, Jundong Xu, Mingzhen Ju, Yue Yu, Chenpeng Wang, Haoxuan Li, Liangming Pan
